extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out => not sent) holds. The only transition that can produce a state with opted_out=true and sent=true is t4 (send_attempt when opted_out and acc==63), since t2 sets sent:=true only under the guard not opted_out (cannot create a violating state). t4 fires only when acc==63. But acc==63 is unreachable: starting from acc=0, each step transition computes acc:=(3*acc + c) mod 64 (the nested if-then-else with constants 2/36/10 is exactly mod-64 reduction, verified for all acc in [0,63]). All additive constants (2, 36, 10) are even and the multiplier 3 is odd, so 3*even+even stays even — acc is an invariant-even value. The reachable acc set is exactly the 32 even values {0,2,...,62}; 63 (odd) is never reachable. Thus t4's guard is never enabled; send_attempt while opted_out always takes t3 (sent:=false), and step events always set sent:=false and never modify opted_out. Exhaustive BFS over the full state space (96 reachable states of (opted_out, sent, acc)) within 66 events finds no state with opted_out and sent both true. Property holds.
